Title: Prolyl 4-hydroxylases, the key enzymes of collagen biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: P4H α(I); P4H α(II); PHY-1; PHY-2; PHY-3
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stabilizes collagen and collagen-like proteins via 4-hydroxyproline formation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed with PHY-1 and PHY-2 in cuticle collagen-synthesizing hypodermal cells in larval stages 1–4 and adults of C. elegans
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Collagens/collagen-like proteins in endoplasmic reticulum/secretory pathway; early embryos in C. elegans
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the β subunit required for assembly of animal collagen P4H complexes; supports active enzyme formation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Protein disulfide isomerase; thioredoxin-like domains a, b, b', a' with catalytic -Cys-Gly-His-Cys- motifs in a and a'
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression/reconstitution of active enzyme complexes; domain-deletion assembly studies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDI serves as the β subunit of vertebrate collagen P4H tetramers. 2. In C. elegans, PDI partners with PHY-1 and PHY-2 to form mixed tetramer and dimeric active P4Hs. 3. The minimum PDI sequence for vertebrate P4H assembly is the b'a' domain pair.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PDI is a structural/assembly partner rather than the catalytic hydroxylase subunit.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Prolyl 4-hydroxylase catalytic α subunit; 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydroxylated proline stabilizes collagen triple helices
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Type I P4H is the main form in most vertebrate cell types and tissues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates proline residues in collagenous -X-Pro-Gly- sequences to form 4-hydroxyproline using Fe2+, 2-oxoglutarate, O2 and ascorbate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for collagen biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Gallus gallus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cloning; recombinant expression with PDI; site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme kinetics; peptide-binding studies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major vertebrate collagen P4H isoform catalyzing collagen proline hydroxylation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Type I collagen P4H isoenzyme branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. α(I) assembles with PDI into [α(I)]2β2 type I P4H tetramers. 2. Type I enzyme is the main P4H form in most tissues. 3. Catalytic residues His412, Asp414, His483, Lys493 and His501 were defined in human α(I). 4. A separate peptide substrate-binding domain was mapped to residues Gly138-Ser244.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Main form in most cell types and tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The α(I) gene is also a hypoxia-inducible target of HIFα.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: normoxia/hypoxia mentioned only for HIF pathway, not collagen catalytic function

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Prolyl 4-hydroxylase catalytic α subunit; 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydroxylated proline stabilizes collagen triple helices
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Major form in chondrocytes, osteoblasts, endothelial cells and epithelial structures
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates proline residues in collagenous peptides to form 4-hydroxyproline
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for collagen biosynthesis in specific tissues
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Mus musculus; Gallus gallus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cloning; recombinant coexpression with PDI; tissue activity measurements; enzyme kinetics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides a tissue-enriched collagen P4H isoform, dominant in cartilage-associated cells
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Type II collagen P4H isoenzyme branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. α(II) assembles with PDI into [α(II)]2β2 type II P4H tetramers. 2. Type II enzyme represents about 30% of total P4H activity in some cultured human cells and 5–15% in chick embryo tissues. 3. It is the major form in chondrocytes, osteoblasts, endothelial cells and epithelial structures, suggesting a major role in cartilage and capillary development.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Major in cultured mouse chondrocytes and cartilage; enriched in chondrocytes, osteoblasts, endothelial and epithelial cells
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows much weaker poly(L-proline) binding than type I enzyme due to differences in peptide-binding domain residues.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Prolyl 4-hydroxylase catalytic α subunit; 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for hydroxylation of cuticle collagens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed in hypodermal cells during larval stages 1–4 and in adults at times of maximal collagen synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates collagen proline residues in cuticle collagen biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant phenotype; recombinant coexpression; in vivo complex analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: phy-1 loss causes dumpy phenotype and reduced total P4H activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential major catalytic component for normal cuticle collagen hydroxylation and nematode morphology
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHY-1 and PHY-2 with PDI assemble into a mixed tetramer plus dimeric forms. 2. In wild type, the PHY-1/PHY-2/PDI2 mixed tetramer is the main form. 3. phy-1 null animals are dumpy and show only partial compensation by PHY-2/PDI dimer, reducing total P4H activity.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Hypodermal cells during L1-L4 and adults
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Can form active αβ dimer with PDI, unlike vertebrate tetramer-only enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Parasite homologs may be useful drug targets

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Prolyl 4-hydroxylase catalytic α subunit; 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for hydroxylation of cuticle collagens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed in hypodermal cells during larval stages 1–4 and in adults at times of maximal collagen synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates collagen proline residues in cuticle collagen biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutant phenotype; recombinant coexpression; in vivo complex analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: phy-2 loss is phenotypically wild type due to compensation by increased PHY-1/PDI dimer assembly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to main mixed tetramer for cuticle collagen hydroxylation, but is partly dispensable due to compensation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHY-2 partners with PHY-1 and PDI in the major mixed tetramer. 2. phy-2 null animals remain phenotypically wild type because increased PHY-1/PDI dimer assembly compensates for loss of the mixed tetramer. 3. PHY-2/PDI dimers are not detected in wild type in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Hypodermal cells during L1-L4 and adults
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Species-specific residues required for mixed tetramer assembly were mapped partly to PHY-2 Asp1-Leu122.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Parasite homologs may be useful drug targets

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Short prolyl 4-hydroxylase catalytic α-like subunit; 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydroxylates proline residues in embryonic collagen-like substrates, likely eggshell collagens
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in embryos, late larval stages and adults; adult expression restricted to spermatheca
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates proline residues in early embryonic collagen-like proteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; recombinant coexpression with specific PDI isoform; mutant metabolite measurement (4-hydroxyproline)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Adult mutants are phenotypically wild type and fertile despite strong embryonic hydroxyproline reduction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly required for hydroxyproline accumulation in early embryos
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHY-3 is a short P4H α-like polypeptide that forms an active enzyme with a distinct C. elegans PDI isoform. 2. phy-3 is not needed for visible adult morphology or fertility. 3. Early mutant embryos show about 90% reduced 4-hydroxyproline, indicating a major role in embryonic collagen-like substrate hydroxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely egg shell collagen-like proteins / embryonic proline-rich proteins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Embryos; late larvae; spermatheca in adults
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Does not associate with the same PDI isoform as PHY-1/PHY-2.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Prolyl 4-hydroxylase catalytic α subunit; 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hydroxylates collagen or other proline-rich proteins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Larvae; later also detected in embryonic mouth-part precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates proline residues in collagen-like or proline-rich substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; recombinant expression with human or Drosophila PDI; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes an active P4H catalytic subunit in Drosophila
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The characterized Drosophila α subunit forms an active tetramer with human or Drosophila PDI. 2. Its mRNA is expressed in larvae and embryonic mouth-part precursors. 3. The large Drosophila P4H family suggests some members may target non-collagen substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Drosophila genome contains 19 α subunit-related genes, many with tissue-specific embryonic expression.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant prolyl 4-hydroxylase; soluble monomeric 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates proline residues in poly(L-proline), (Pro-Pro-Gly)10 and other proline-containing peptides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; recombinant enzyme assay; substrate specificity and kinetic analyses; sequencing of hydroxylated products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes broad proline hydroxylation with unusual ability to accept both poly(L-proline) and collagen-like peptides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At-P4H-1 is a 283-residue soluble monomer, unlike animal tetrameric collagen P4Hs. 2. It effectively hydroxylates poly(L-proline) and (Pro-Pro-Gly)10. 3. It preferentially hydroxylates prolines preceding glycine in (Pro-Pro-Gly)10 and also accepts peptides containing only one proline, indicating no strict requirement for a poly(L-proline) II helix.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of six Arabidopsis P4H-like genes reported in the genome.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Viral prolyl 4-hydroxylase; soluble monomeric 2-oxoglutarate/non-heme Fe(II)-dependent dioxygenase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates proline residues in (Pro-Pro-Gly)10, poly(L-proline), and viral repeat peptides such as (Pro-Ala-Pro-Lys)n
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; recombinant enzyme assays; substrate profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hydroxylates multiple viral proline-rich repeat substrates and collagen-like peptides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBCV-1 P4H is a 210-residue soluble monomer. 2. It hydroxylates both poly(L-proline) and collagen-like peptide (Pro-Pro-Gly)10. 3. It also hydroxylates viral repeat peptides, with preference for prolines preceding alanine in (Pro-Ala-Pro-Lys)n.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike animal and Arabidopsis P4Hs, no asymmetric hydroxylation pattern was observed in (Pro-Ala-Pro-Lys)n.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Caenorhabditis elegans; Drosophila melanogaster
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: oxygen-dependent degradation of HIFα affecting glycolytic enzyme gene expression and other hypoxia responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes hydroxylation-dependent recognition and degradation of HIFα, thereby repressing hypoxia-inducible genes under normoxia
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HIF P4Hs are a novel cytoplasmic P4H family distinct from collagen P4Hs. 2. They hydroxylate a critical proline in HIFα under normoxia. 3. Hydroxylation enables VHL E3 ligase binding and proteasomal degradation of HIFα, suppressing hypoxia-induced genes including glycolytic enzymes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is regulatory control of hypoxia-response metabolism rather than direct nutrient metabolite biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Homo sapiens; Caenorhabditis elegans; Drosophila melanogaster
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions upstream of VHL E3 ubiquitin ligase complex recognition of HIFα
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls hypoxia adaptation including glycolytic gene induction
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active under normoxic conditions; hydroxylation ceases in hypoxia
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses HIF-dependent metabolic and hypoxia gene programs under normoxia
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of hypoxia-inducible metabolic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical hydroxylation assays; binding/degradation studies; sequence comparison

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Hydroxylates HIFα homolog to promote normoxic repression of hypoxia-induced genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EGL-9 is the C. elegans HIF P4H. 2. It belongs to the HIF P4H family distinct from collagen P4Hs. 3. It is implicated in oxygen-dependent regulation of HIF signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a named member of the HIF P4H family in this review; direct nutrient metabolite link is indirect via hypoxia-regulated metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts upstream of VHL-like degradation pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as oxygen-dependent negative regulator of HIF signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential oxygen sensor for HIF pathway in nematode
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Family identification and comparative characterization in cited studies
